title: Chess Grandmaster Training Simulator
description: A ReadyPlayer.me avatar stands beside a life-sized chess board from ABeautifulGame. The user must drag specific chess pieces to target squares following a famous opening sequence, while the avatar reacts with animations to each correct or incorrect move. After completing the sequence, the board evaluates the final position and displays a score.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements a chess training simulator. Load ABeautifulGame.glb as a full chess set placed on a table-height platform at the scene center. Load readyplayer.me.glb as a humanoid avatar standing to the right of the board, scaled so the avatar appears life-sized relative to the board. Use a PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 3, 5) looking at the board center. Add an ambient light (intensity 0.6), a directional light from above-left casting soft shadows, and a warm point light near the avatar. Enable OrbitControls with constrained vertical rotation (10-80 degrees) and limited zoom range. Implement raycasting-based drag-and-drop: the user clicks a chess piece, it highlights with an emissive glow, then the user clicks a target square to move it there. The piece animates smoothly to the new position using a tween over 0.4 seconds. Define a 6-move opening sequence (e.g., Italian Game: e4, e5, Nf3, Nc6, Bc4, Bc5). Display the current expected move in a HUD overlay at the top. Validate each move: if correct, the avatar plays a nodding animation (rotate head mesh down and up) and a green particle burst appears. If incorrect, the avatar shakes its head side-to-side and the piece returns to its original position with a red flash. Track move count, mistakes, and elapsed time. After all 6 moves are completed correctly, display a translucent overlay panel showing total time, mistake count, and a star rating (3 stars for 0 mistakes, 2 for 1-2, 1 for 3+). Add a reset button in the bottom-right corner that restores all pieces to starting positions. The board squares should subtly highlight valid target squares when a piece is selected. Use a gradient skybox or simple environment background. Ensure both GLB models load with a visible loading progress bar before the simulation begins.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  currentMoveIndex: number,         // index of the current expected move in the sequence (0-5)
  correctMovesCount: number,         // total number of correct moves made so far
  incorrectMovesCount: number,         // total number of incorrect moves attempted
  isDragging: boolean,         // whether a chess piece is currently being dragged
  selectedPieceId: string,         // identifier of the currently selected chess piece or empty string
  avatarAnimationState: string,         // current avatar animation (idle|nodding|shaking|celebrating)
  boardEvaluationScore: number,         // calculated board position score after sequence completion
  sequenceCompleted: boolean,         // whether all 6 moves have been completed correctly
  particleEffectActive: boolean,         // whether particle effect is currently playing
  cameraDistance: number,         // current distance of camera from board center in units
}
